publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-07-27 00:07:29 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: William Henry Miller miscellany
did.abstract: Includes drawings and blueprints for Cornell buildings, including Sage infirmary medical building and the Cascadilla School in Ithaca, New York; unidentified drawings; details of two fireplaces in the Ithaca Conservatory of Music (now Ithaca College); two small hand carved wooden panels presented by Miller to the College of Architecture at the time of its semi-centennial (1921); a scrapbook of clippings and plates from architectural magazines; a book of architectural postcards, including some depicting Miller's buildings; account books with Gauntlett and Brooks, pertaining to the Fiske House of Jennie McGraw Fiske; two letters from Andrew Dickson White to Miller, 1870, 1896; and correspondence relating to the construction of the kitchen in Prudence Risley Hall, including letters to E.L. Williams (Cornell University Treasurer) from Miller, Thomas Tree (Manager of Sage College), and Flora Rose, 1913.
Also, plans and specifications for addition to President White's house, 1895, with comments by Andrew Dickson White (12 items).
A letter possibly to William Henry Miller from his mother, 1879.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Jude Corina & Lin Sen Chai, December 2017

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: William Henry Miller was an Ithaca, New York architect. He designed several Cornell University buildings, including Uris Library. He was a member of the Cornell Class of 1872.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes drawings and blueprints for Cornell buildings, including Sage infirmary medical building and the Cascadilla School in Ithaca, New York; unidentified drawings; details of two fireplaces in the Ithaca Conservatory of Music (now Ithaca College); two small hand carved wooden panels presented by Miller to the College of Architecture at the time of its semi-centennial (1921); a scrapbook of clippings and plates from architectural magazines; a book of architectural postcards, including some depicting Miller's buildings; account books with Gauntlett and Brooks, pertaining to the Fiske House of Jennie McGraw Fiske; two letters from Andrew Dickson White to Miller, 1870, 1896; and correspondence relating to the construction of the kitchen in Prudence Risley Hall, including letters to E.L. Williams (Cornell University Treasurer) from Miller, Thomas Tree (Manager of Sage College), and Flora Rose, 1913.
Also, plans and specifications for addition to President White's house, 1895, with comments by Andrew Dickson White (12 items).
A letter possibly to William Henry Miller from his mother, 1879.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also: William Henry Miller papers, #1856.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to building plans in Mapcase Folder 1 and 6 restricted to permission of the office of Planning Design and Construction.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Collection originally found to contain mold. Material was cleaned, but mold may still be present. Individuals with weak immune systems may need to take extra precautions.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: William Henry Miller Miscellany, #2811. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
